---
title: "Impact Evaluation in Practice Practice Implications"
description: "Practice synthesis for Impact Evaluation in Practice."
book_id: "impact-evaluation-in-practice"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Practice"
citation_key: "gertler_martinez_premand_rawlings_vermeersch_2016_impact_evaluation_in_practice"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:impact-evaluation-in-practice:what-is-impact-evaluation:c0001"
  - "chunk:impact-evaluation-in-practice:constructing-a-theory-of-change:c0001"
  - "chunk:impact-evaluation-in-practice:causal-inference:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This practice synthesis draws on selected notes from *Impact Evaluation in Practice* [@gertler_martinez_premand_rawlings_vermeersch_2016_impact_evaluation_in_practice].

## Evaluation Practice Implications

- Clarify whether the question is descriptive, implementation-focused, or causal.
- Build the evaluation around a theory of change before selecting an identification strategy.
- Assess whether program rules, timing, and eligibility create feasible comparison options.
- Plan data and sample size early enough to protect credibility.

## Applying the Notes

Use these implications as prompts during scoping, design review, method selection, and reporting. They should be adapted to the evaluation's decision context, available evidence, and user needs.

## Links

- [What Is Impact Evaluation?](section-01-what-is-impact-evaluation.qmd)
- [Constructing a Theory of Change](section-02-constructing-theory-of-change.qmd)
- [Causal Inference and Counterfactuals](section-03-causal-inference-counterfactuals.qmd)

## References
